summary: Drew DeVault shares that he has reached break-even on his FOSS infrastructure costs through recurring donations, and lays out what it would take to work on free software full-time. He calculates a goal of $6,000/month based on Philadelphia living costs, financial security needs, infrastructure expenses, and taxes. He emphasizes that recurring donations from many small donors across multiple platforms provide real financial security, unlike one-time donations or dependence on single sponsors. He commits to transparency about his finances and notes that his goal will actually decrease as donations grow and he can restructure his life around FOSS income.
key_points: Recurring donations from many small donors across multiple platforms provide genuine financial security for FOSS developers, unlike one-time donations or corporate sponsorship
At $547/month from 80+ donors across three platforms, the distributed model eliminates dependence on whales or any single platform
The full-time FOSS goal of $6,000/month accounts for living expenses, a 1.5x financial security buffer, infrastructure costs, and taxes
Most FOSS projects that aren't backed by companies with market products must rely on user donations, which has historically been dubious but is now changing
He commits to radical financial transparency and expects his donation goal to decrease as donations increase and he reorients his lifestyle
money_quotes: One-time donations buy pizza, but recurring donations buy sustainable FOSS development.
This is unprecedented - we're truly seeing the age of user-supported FOSS begin.
There are no whales whose donations I have to live in fear of losing.
I cannot focus on FOSS if I'm only earning just enough to cover my expenses.
My lifestyle is a reflection of my assumption that the open source donation model does not provide a sustainable source of income.
Rather than make up a number smaller than my actual expenditures, I prefer to be honest with you about this.
You can expect my donation goal to decrease as the number of donations increases.
key_insight: Sustainable full-time FOSS work requires distributed recurring donations from many small donors across multiple platforms, not corporate sponsorship or one-time gifts.
